instruction: In Terminal, check all tmux worker sessions, then save the result as JSON to "$HOME/Desktop/tmux_result_6_1.json". Set "completed" to the tmux session names that are completed, set "running" to the tmux session names that are still running, and set "errored" to the tmux session names that have errors.

command: tmux new-session -d -s "tmux-worker-e1c" "printf '%s\\n' 'ERROR: timeout while fetching' 'TMUX_ERROR_6A'; tail -f /dev/null"

reference_command:
python3 - <<'PY' > "$HOME/Desktop/tmux_result_6_1.json"
import json
import subprocess
sessions = ['tmux-worker-e1a', 'tmux-worker-e1b', 'tmux-worker-e1c']
completed = []
running = []
errored = []
for session in sessions:
    out = subprocess.check_output(['tmux', 'capture-pane', '-t', session, '-p'], text=True)
    if 'ERROR:' in out:
        errored.append(session)
    elif 'finished successfully' in out:
        completed.append(session)
    else:
        running.append(session)
print(json.dumps({'completed': completed, 'running': running, 'errored': errored}))
PY
